spec_match.rationale: The page now matches the ChromeStatus framing: a Windows-only TCP port randomization change with no JavaScript API surface, demonstrated through collision and retry-pressure simulation.

interactive.rationale: DevTools MCP exercised the strategy select, connection-rate slider, TIME_WAIT slider, port-range select, and Simulate button. The controls update the live canvas, first-collision, total-collision, peak-in-flight, busy-pick-share, and diagnostic readouts.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede and diagnostic copy now explain both the birthday-collision rollout constraint and the side-channel motivation ChromeStatus describes.

edge_cases.rationale: The simulator surfaces boundary range saturation, sequential predictability, random retry pressure, and RFC 6056-style scatter behavior.

references.rationale: The page cites the required chromestatus.com/feature/5106900286570496 self-heal link.

design_system.rationale: CSS uses shared design variables, button background and color are paired, canvas colors are read from the design-system variables, and the form controls now have associated labels.

summary: Re-reviewed in Chrome 148 with chrome-devtools-mcp. The simulator auto-renders a nonblank canvas and live stats, the ChromeStatus link is present, strategy/range changes plus the rate and TIME_WAIT sliders update the model, a high-pressure sequential run reports the expected saturation diagnostic, and the conformance route reported 6 pass / 0 fail. DevTools initially surfaced missing label associations on four controls; the page now associates each visible label with its select or slider. Final console and network checks were clean.
